question: When does my policy renew?
answer: Your policy renewal date is shown on your policy schedule. We will contact you at least 21 days before renewal with your new terms and premium.

question: How do I make a claim?
answer: You can make a claim by calling our claims line on 0800 123 4567, through the customer portal, or by asking me to escalate to our claims team.

question: What does my home insurance cover?
answer: Our Home & Contents policy covers the structure of your home (buildings) and your personal belongings (contents) against risks including fire, flood, theft, and accidental damage. Check your policy schedule for specific cover limits.

question: How do I change my address?
answer: You can update your address through the customer portal or by contacting us. Please note that address changes may affect your premium as risk factors vary by location.

question: Why has my premium gone up?
answer: Premiums are reviewed at renewal based on several factors: claims history, changes in risk assessment, inflation in repair/rebuild costs, insurance premium tax changes, and market conditions. Your renewal letter explains the specific factors for your policy.

question: What is the excess on my policy?
answer: Your excess is the amount you pay towards any claim. It is shown on your policy schedule. Typical excesses are between 100 and 500 GBP depending on your policy type and cover level.

question: Can I cancel my policy?
answer: You can cancel your policy at any time. If you cancel within the first 14 days (cooling-off period), you receive a full refund. After 14 days, cancellation fees may apply. Contact us to discuss your options.

question: How do I reset my portal password?
answer: Click 'Forgot Password' on the login page, enter your registered email, and follow the reset link. If you are still having trouble, our technical support team can help.

question: What are my rights under GDPR?
answer: Under GDPR, you have the right to access your personal data, request correction of inaccurate data, request deletion (right to be forgotten), restrict processing, data portability, and object to processing. To exercise any of these rights, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@meridian-insurance.co.uk or ask to speak with our compliance team.

question: How long do you keep my data?
answer: We retain your policy data for 7 years after your policy ends, as required by FCA regulations. Claims data is retained for 7 years after settlement. Marketing preferences are kept until you withdraw consent. You can request a full data retention schedule from our compliance team.

question: Who do you share my data with?
answer: We share data with reinsurers (to underwrite your policy), fraud prevention agencies (CIFAS, Insurance Fraud Bureau), credit reference agencies (for payment checks), and our approved repair network (when processing claims). We never sell your data to third parties for marketing purposes.

question: How does the claims process work?
answer: Step 1: Report your claim via phone, portal, or chat. Step 2: We assign an adjuster within 48 hours. Step 3: The adjuster assesses damage (may require a site visit). Step 4: We confirm your settlement amount. Step 5: Payment is issued within 5 working days of approval. Complex claims may take longer — your adjuster will keep you updated throughout.

question: Why can I not log into the portal?
answer: Common login issues: (1) Check you are using your registered email address. (2) Clear your browser cache and cookies. (3) Try a different browser or incognito mode. (4) If your account is locked after 5 failed attempts, wait 30 minutes or use 'Forgot Password'. If none of these work, our technical support team can investigate further.

question: How do I upload documents to my claim?
answer: Log into the customer portal, navigate to 'My Claims', select your active claim, and click 'Upload Documents'. We accept PDF, JPG, and PNG files up to 10MB each. You can also email documents to claims-docs@meridian-insurance.co.uk quoting your claim reference number.

question: What payment methods do you accept?
answer: We accept direct debit (monthly or annual), debit card, and credit card payments. Monthly direct debit instalments include a small credit charge (equivalent to 4.5% APR). Paying annually by direct debit saves you 3% compared to monthly payments.

question: When is my next payment due?
answer: Your payment schedule is shown in the customer portal under 'Billing'. Direct debit payments are collected on the same date each month (shown on your schedule). If your payment date falls on a weekend or bank holiday, it is collected on the next working day.

question: What is the cooling-off period?
answer: You have 14 days from the start of your policy (or from when you receive your documents, whichever is later) to cancel without penalty. During this period you receive a full refund minus any days of cover already used. After 14 days, our standard cancellation terms apply.

question: What discounts are available?
answer: We offer several discounts: no-claims discount (up to 30% for 5+ claim-free years), multi-policy discount (10% when you bundle home and motor), security discount (5% for approved alarm systems or smart locks), and annual payment discount (3% versus monthly instalments). Discounts are applied automatically where eligible.
